title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lucy and Miss Marple discuss the series of deaths involving Alfred, Harold, and a woman impersonating Martina. Lucy identifies Sedgwick and Brian Easton as the remaining potential suspects. Lucy recalls a memory of Mrs. Stott-Wester asking about a tall, dark-haired man on a balcony; Lucy suspects Brian might be that man, noting that his golden hair appeared black when he wore something on his head. Miss Marple suggests a motive: if Alexander meets with misfortune before turning twenty-one, Brian, as his father and closest relative, would inherit the money. While Lucy finds the idea of Brian killing his own child for money abhorrent, Miss Marple notes that people often commit abominable crimes for financial gain.

Lucy shares her observations of the family members, noting that Cecil is charming but potentially manipulative and seems indifferent to the deaths of Alfred and Harold, focusing instead on obtaining money for Rutherford Hall. She describes Mr. Esterly as melancholic. Regarding Brian, Lucy notes his unrealistic plans for Rutherford Manor, which he favors because it resembles a Victorian building from his childhood. Lucy also suspects Brian may have lied about his travel; although Emma picked him up from a 4:50 pm train from Paddington, Lucy believes he may have taken an earlier train. Miss Marple asserts that Brian did indeed arrive by train. Miss Marple concludes that the killer will not stop until they commit another crime and expresses confidence that the arrival of Mrs. McGillicuddy will be important.
